name: Holy Redeemer Community Of Se Wis.
description_short: Holy Redeemer Community Credit Union is a FISCU credit union serving 243 members with $745,711 in assets, designated as a low-income institution in Milwaukee, Wisconsin.
description_long:
Holy Redeemer Community of SE Wisconsin is a federally insured credit union (FISCU) chartered under NCUA Region 3 with charter number 68044. The institution was established to serve low-income communities in the Milwaukee area and maintains its headquarters at 3500 W Mother Daniels Way. With a founding mission centered on community financial access, the credit union operates as a member-owned cooperative financial institution.

The credit union offers standard banking services including member savings accounts, lending products, and member services through its single branch location. Members can access services during limited hours: Sundays 9-11:30 AM, Wednesdays 4-7 PM, and Fridays. The institution maintains a lean operational structure with 1 full-time employee and 3 part-time employees, managed by President John Daniels III and Manager Lisa Neal.

What distinguishes Holy Redeemer is its explicit low-income designation and community-focused mission serving southeastern Wisconsin. The credit union demonstrates strong financial stability with a Net Worth Ratio of 29.25%, well above the 7.0% regulatory minimum, indicating a solid safety cushion for members' deposits. Recent loan growth of 45.45% over four quarters shows active engagement in lending to its membership base, while maintaining a conservative Loan-to-Share Ratio of 30.39%.

However, the institution faces real operational constraints. With only 243 members and $745.7K in total assets, it operates at a small scale with limited staffing and restricted service hours. The institution experienced member decline of 5.08% and asset decline of 7.63% over the past four quarters, suggesting shrinking reach. The credit union has zero member reviews on the platform, and its profitability metric (ROAA of 1.95%) is modest. Prospective members should verify current service offerings and accessibility before joining. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Lending for members (45.45% quarterly growth indicates active loan origination)
Member financial access for low-income communities
pros: Low-income designated institution explicitly serving underbanked communities in Milwaukee
Strong Net Worth Ratio of 29.25%, well above the 7.0% regulatory minimum, ensuring member deposit safety
Impressive loan growth of 45.45% over four quarters, indicating active lending commitment
Federally insured FISCU status provides NCUA protection up to standard insurance limits
Member-owned cooperative structure aligns profits with member interests
Conservative Loan-to-Share Ratio of 30.39% indicates responsible lending and liquidity management
Weekend hours (Sunday 9-11:30 AM) accommodates members with traditional work schedules
cons: Extremely limited service hours (Sunday, Wednesday, Friday only) restrict member access compared to typical banks
Very small institution with only 243 members and $745.7K in assets limits product sophistication and service variety
Declining membership (-5.08% over four quarters) and shrinking assets (-7.63%) suggest institutional challenges
Only 1 full-time employee and 3 part-time staff creates operational and service delivery constraints
Zero member reviews available, making it difficult to assess actual member satisfaction and service quality
best_for: Low-income Milwaukee residents seeking accessible community credit union services aligned with their financial situation
Members prioritizing deposit safety and stable institutions with strong capitalization ratios
Individuals able to access services during limited weekend and weekday evening hours
diagnosis: Holy Redeemer Community Credit Union is best suited for low-income Milwaukee residents who can work with its restrictive service hours and small operational scale, and who prioritize community-focused, stable banking with strong regulatory capital. The main caveat is that severely limited hours (Sunday, Wednesday, Friday only) and minimal staffing may create significant accessibility barriers for members with typical work schedules.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
